interpretation: TBCEL, or Tubulin-specific chaperone cofactor E-like protein, is involved in the regulation of tubulin stability, which is crucial for maintaining the structural integrity of microtubules in cells. While there is no direct evidence linking TBCEL to Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head (SONFH), its role in cellular stability and function could imply a potential indirect connection, particularly in the context of steroid-induced cellular stress and bone health. The retrieved evidence primarily discusses TBCEL's general functions and associations with other conditions, but does not provide strong support for its involvement in SONFH. Therefore, the clinical relevance of TBCEL as a biomarker for early detection of SONFH remains weak, as the evidence does not specifically address its role in this disease context.
relevance_summary: TBCEL's role in tubulin stability may suggest a potential, albeit weak, connection to the cellular mechanisms involved in Steroid-Induced Osteonecrosis of the Femoral Head.
citations: TBCEL ↔ inborn disorder of amino acid metabolism. https://platform.opentargets.org/target/ENSG00000154114/associations

Disease: inborn disorder of amino acid metabolism

result.title: TBCEL ↔ allergic urticaria
result.text:
Disease: allergic urticaria
Description: commonly referred to as hives, is a kind of skin rash notable for pale red, raised, itchy bumps. Hives may cause a burning or stinging sensation. Histamine and other proinflammatory substances are released from mast cells in the skin and tissues in response to the binding of allergen-bound IgE antibodies to high-affinity cell surface receptors. Basophils and other inflammatory cells are also seen to release histamine and other mediators, and are thought to play an important role, especially in chronic urticarial diseases.
Association score: 0.136

result.text:
Disease: hyperaldosteronism
Description: Overproduction of aldosterone by the adrenal glands, which may lead to hypokalemia and/or hypernatremia. [ NCIt:P378 ]
Association score: 0.113
